schema.ion.id › core › reconcile › v1

core/reconcile/v1

Pack IRI: https://schema.ion.id/core/reconcile/v1#  |  Layer: L4 — Cross-sector core  |  Version: v1

context.jsonld attributes.yaml vocab.jsonld

Schemas in this pack

IONReconcileAttributes

IRI: https://schema.ion.id/core/reconcile/v1#IONReconcileAttributes

PropertyMandatoryDescription
reconId—ION-assigned unique identifier for this reconciliation session.
contractId—The trade contract this reconciliation covers.
settlementBasis—The event that triggers the settlement window countdown. Agreed at order init and locked at confirm.
settlementWindow—Time window after settlementBasis event within which settlement must be completed. ISO 8601 duration.
collectedBy—Which participant collected payment from consumer.
amounts—Declared amounts for reconciliation.
returnWindowExpiry—Date-time when return window closes and withholding amount releases. After this date, withholdingAmount is settled to BP
adjustments—Contract-level adjustments to the base order amount that must be agreed before final settlement. These are post-confirm
netSettlementAmount—Final net amount to settle to BPP/receiver after all adjustments. Computed: baseContractAmount - finderFee - withholding
netWithholdingRelease—Withholding amount to release to BPP after return window. withholdingAmount - returnDeductions - refunds
counterparty—Identification of the counterparty in this reconciliation.
bankDetails—Settlement bank account details for the receiver.
reconStatus—PENDING: recon initiated, awaiting counterparty acknowledgment. AGREED: both parties agree on amounts. DISPUTED: amounts
disputeReason—Human-readable reason for DISPUTED status. Referenced when raising a dispute ticket via raise/on_raise.
recon_status—Numeric recon status compatible with ONDC RSF receiver_recon model. 01=amounts match, 02=collector paid more than expect
slaPenaltyDeduction—Amount deducted from BPP's net settlement due to confirmed SLA breaches on this contract. BAP tracks breaches in perform
slaPenaltyBreakdown—Per-breach penalty calculation for transparency.
streamerCommissionAmount—Amount to settle to streamer/creator for B2C-LIVE orders. Deducted from BPP or BAP per commercial agreement.
affiliateCommissionAmount—Amount to settle to affiliate for affiliate-sourced orders.
isBatch—Whether this reconcile covers multiple contracts as a batch. Marketplace scale requires batched settlement (daily, weekl
contractIds—List of contract IDs covered when isBatch=true. Single-contract reconcile populates contractId instead.
batchPeriod—Time window this batch covers.
contractDetails—Per-contract financial breakdown for batch reconcile.
parentReconId—When this reconcile corrects an earlier one, references the earlier reconId. Used for clawbacks, additional payments, po
adjustmentType—Type of adjustment being made against a prior reconcile. CLAWBACK: recover funds previously settled incorrectly. ADDITIO
adjustmentReason—Plain-language reason for the adjustment
adjustmentEvidence—Evidence URLs supporting the adjustment (audit logs, screenshots, communications)
taxWithholdings—Indonesian tax withholdings applied to this settlement. Marketplaces in Indonesia withhold PPh 22 on certain transaction
codRemittance—COD remittance record — how the collecting party (LSP or BPP) remits collected cash to the selling party. Batched across

Pack README

ion-core/reconcile/v1

Inter-NP financial reconciliation between BAP (collector) and BPP (receiver).

Attaches to

`beckn:Settlement.settlementAttributes`

APIs

`reconcile` → `on_reconcile`

Flow

1. BAP initiates `/reconcile` with contract amounts, finder fee, withholding, adjustments

2. BPP responds via `/on_reconcile` with `recon_status`: 01=AGREED, 02=OVERPAID, 03=UNDERPAID

3. If DISPUTED, either party raises a ticket via `raise/on_raise`

Adjustments

Adjustments cover contract-level changes that must be agreed before settlement:

PRICE_ADJUSTMENT, CANCELLATION_FEE, RETURN_DEDUCTION, DAMAGE_PENALTY, COD_REMITTANCE, REFUND

Relationship to ONDC RSF

reconcile/on_reconcile maps conceptually to ONDC RSF `recon/on_recon`.

The adjustment object is an ION addition — ONDC RSF does not define adjustment types at the protocol level.

Part of schema.ion.id — ION Network vocabulary. Built from schema/extensions/core/reconcile/v1/ in the ion-network spec. Generated 2026-04-27 18:43 UTC.
